race_name: UCI Gran Fondo Hangzhou Qiandao Lake

top_categories.workout_context: 5,906ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Race-pace efforts that mimic UCI Gran Fondo Hangzhou Qiandao Lake's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before May.
66 miles on flat burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
UCI Gran Fondo Hangzhou Qiandao Lake's flat forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 66 miles.
Base fitness for UCI Gran Fondo Hangzhou Qiandao Lake. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
89ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
At UCI Gran Fondo Hangzhou Qiandao Lake, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At UCI Gran Fondo Hangzhou Qiandao Lake, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of UCI Gran Fondo Hangzhou Qiandao Lake. Train it until it feels boring.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on flat. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 5,906ft of climbing on flat does to your power file on race day.
5,906ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.

race_overlay.heat: May in Qiandao Lake, Chun’an County, Hangzhou, Zhejiang Province, China can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before UCI Gran Fondo Hangzhou Qiandao Lake. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for UCI Gran Fondo Hangzhou Qiandao Lake’s 66 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.
race_overlay.altitude: UCI Gran Fondo Hangzhou Qiandao Lake with 5,906ft of climbing, much of it above 8,000ft demands altitude preparation. Arrive 5–7 days early for acclimatization. Expect 5–15% power reduction at altitude. Increase iron intake 4 weeks out. Hydrate aggressively — altitude increases fluid loss by 20–40%.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at UCI Gran Fondo Hangzhou Qiandao Lake requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on flat to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on VO2max, Race Simulation, and Durability to prepare you for 66 miles of flat in Qiandao Lake, Chun’an County, Hangzhou, Zhejiang Province, China.
